req.title: System-Admin creates onboarding-task
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to create a onboarding-task through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: This test suite validates the system-admin onboarding-task creation capability across positive, negative, and boundary scenarios. Test design applies Equivalence Partitioning to separate valid/invalid inputs, Boundary Value Analysis for field length limits, Negative Path testing for authentication/authorization failures, and Decision Table logic for mandatory field validation. All tests are deterministic, self-contained, and idempotent via UUID generation and test data isolation.

tests.name: test_admin_creates_onboarding_task_success
preconditions: system-admin is authenticated with valid JWT token
actions: Construct valid onboarding-task payload with all mandatory fields: title, description, assignee_id, due_date
POST request to /onboarding-tasks with Authorization header
Capture response status code and body
expected: Response body contains 'id' field with UUID format
Response body contains 'status' field with value 'created'
Audit record written to audit_log table with event_type='ONBOARDING_TASK_CREATED'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_admin_creates_onboarding_task_success(admin_token, api_base_url):
    task_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "title": f"Onboarding Task {task_id}",
        "description": "Complete orientation program",
        "assignee_id": "user-123",
        "due_date": (datetime.now() + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat()
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/onboarding-tasks",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
    data = resp.json()
    assert "id" in data
    assert data.get("status") == "created"
    assert <check_audit_record>(data["id"], "ONBOARDING_TASK_CREATED")

tests.name: test_admin_missing_mandatory_field_title
preconditions: system-admin is authenticated with valid JWT token
actions: Construct onboarding-task payload omitting mandatory 'title' field
Populate all other mandatory fields: description, assignee_id, due_date
POST request to /onboarding-tasks with Authorization header
Capture response status code and validation error details
expected: Response body contains error message mentioning 'title' field is required
Response body contains 'errors' array with validation error details
No onboarding-task record created in database
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_admin_missing_mandatory_field_title(admin_token, api_base_url):
    payload = {
        "description": "Complete orientation program",
        "assignee_id": "user-123",
        "due_date": (datetime.now() + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat()
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/onboarding-tasks",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    data = resp.json()
    assert "errors" in data
    error_msg = str(data["errors"]).lower()
    assert "title" in error_msg and "required" in error_msg
    assert <verify_no_record_created>()

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_task
actions: Construct valid onboarding-task payload with all mandatory fields
POST request to /onboarding-tasks WITHOUT Authorization header
Capture response status code
expected: Response body contains 'Unauthorized' or 'Authentication required' message
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_task(api_base_url):
    payload = {
        "title": "Onboarding Task",
        "description": "Complete orientation",
        "assignee_id": "user-123",
        "due_date": (datetime.now() + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat()
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/onboarding-tasks",
        json=payload
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    data = resp.json()
    assert any(kw in data.get("message", "").lower() 
               for kw in ["unauthorized", "authentication"])

tests.name: test_non_admin_role_forbidden_create_task
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
actions: Construct valid onboarding-task payload with all mandatory fields
POST request to /onboarding-tasks with non-admin user token
Capture response status code
expected: Response body contains 'Forbidden' or 'Insufficient permissions' message

tests.name: test_title_field_boundary_max_length
preconditions: system-admin is authenticated with valid JWT token
Title field has maximum length constraint (e.g., 255 characters)
actions: Construct onboarding-task payload with title at maximum allowed length (255 chars)
Populate all other mandatory fields
POST request to /onboarding-tasks with Authorization header
Capture response status code and body
expected: Onboarding-task created with full title value preserved
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_title_field_boundary_max_length(admin_token, api_base_url):
    max_title = "A" * 255
    task_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "title": max_title,
        "description": "Complete orientation",
        "assignee_id": "user-123",
        "due_date": (datetime.now() + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat()
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/onboarding-tasks",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
    data = resp.json()
    assert data.get("title") == max_title
    assert <check_audit_record>(data["id"], "ONBOARDING_TASK_CREATED")

tests.name: test_title_field_exceeds_max_length_rejected
preconditions: system-admin is authenticated with valid JWT token
Title field has maximum length constraint (e.g., 255 characters)
actions: Construct onboarding-task payload with title exceeding maximum length (256+ chars)
Populate all other mandatory fields
POST request to /onboarding-tasks with Authorization header
Capture response status code and validation error
expected: Response body contains validation error mentioning title length exceeded
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_title_field_exceeds_max_length_rejected(admin_token, api_base_url):
    oversized_title = "A" * 256
    payload = {
        "title": oversized_title,
        "description": "Complete orientation",
        "assignee_id": "user-123",
        "due_date": (datetime.now() + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat()
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/onboarding-tasks",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    data = resp.json()
    assert "errors" in data
    error_msg = str(data["errors"]).lower()
    assert "title" in error_msg and "length" in error_msg

tests.name: test_due_date_invalid_format_rejected
preconditions: system-admin is authenticated with valid JWT token
actions: Construct onboarding-task payload with due_date in invalid format (e.g., 'invalid-date')
Populate all other mandatory fields
POST request to /onboarding-tasks with Authorization header
Capture response status code and validation error
expected: Response body contains validation error mentioning invalid date format
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_due_date_invalid_format_rejected(admin_token, api_base_url):
    payload = {
        "title": "Onboarding Task",
        "description": "Complete orientation",
        "assignee_id": "user-123",
        "due_date": "invalid-date-format"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/onboarding-tasks",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    data = resp.json()
    assert "errors" in data
    error_msg = str(data["errors"]).lower()
    assert "due_date" in error_msg and "format" in error_msg
